Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16
Off Calendar Dec-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David W. Chen, Esq. of Stratman Patterson & Hunter, (510-457-3440), for Alan Abruzzo, Sara Corbett and Tiya-B Nucci, Defendants and moving parties.
Mark W. Hooshmand, Esq. of Hooshmand Law Group, (415-318-5709), for Thomas Wong, Plaintiff and opposing party.
Reporter Judith A. DeAlba, CSR #5709, (650-571-1473).

Both parties submit to the the tentative ruling on defendants' motion to strike. Tentative ruling is now adopted as follows: DEFENDANTs ALAN ABRUZZO, SARA CORBETT and TIYA-B NUCCI's Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Complaint Pursuant To CCP Section 425.16 is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden aND show that Plaintiff's complaint arises out of protected activity. "Terminating a tenancy or removing a property from the rental market are not activities taken in furtherance of the constitutional rights of petition or free speech. "Clark v. Mazgani (2009)170 Cal. App. 4th 1281, 1286-87.

Court responds to the inquiry as to the ruling on sanctions as follows: Court leans to deny the sanction requested by Plaintiff. Court instructs Plaintiff to file its motion to hear the sanctions issue. Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Reporter Judith A. DeAlba, CSR #5709, D501.
